mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals near-term consumption strength, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses below 0.25% or stays below it for several months, discount-rate pressure is low and liquidity support raises S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% or remains below 0.25% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered low-rate episodes do not have higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered episodes, or if the triggered hit rate for positive returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment greater than 0.3 percentage point over 3 months signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage point over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if conditional mean returns are not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid CPI gains while the policy rate remains near zero signal that monetary policy is behind inflation, raising future tightening risk and lowering S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot CPIAUCSL was 237.566 and FEDFUNDS was 0.14
regime_hint: inflation pressure with policy rate constrained near zero
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-high and FEDFUNDS-low interaction has nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if its coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent 3-month gains in industrial production and payroll employment confirm a real activity recovery, improving earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both rise over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot INDPRO was 107.0793 and PAYEMS was 142371.0
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous INDPRO and PAYEMS gains are not followed by higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than single-series gains, or if the interaction term is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts decline more than 10% below its 12-month average signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction softness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST weakness is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payrolls paired with a falling unemployment rate signals broad labor-market improvement that supports household income and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest snapshot PAYEMS was 142371.0 and UNRATE was 5.1
failure_condition: Reject if months with PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline do not produce higher 5-day S&P 500 returns than months with only one labor series improving.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in CPI raises expected discount rates and margin pressure, reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if accelerating CPI is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or if the walk-forward CPI acceleration coefficient is not negative.
